questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: These apples are too expensive to be bad.
Conclusions: I. When apples are in short supply, they go up.
II. The higher the selling price, the superior is the quality of the commodity.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: In deserts, camels are indispensable for people to travel from one place to another.
Conclusions: I. Camels are the only cheapest mode of transport available in deserts.
II. There are plenty of camels in deserts.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Of all the television set manufacturers in India, the 'Stellar' has the largest sale.
Conclusions: I. The volume of sales of all the brands of television manufactured in India is known.
II. The production of no other television set in India is as large as that of 'Stellar'.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: I. The TV programmes telecasted specially for women are packed with a variety of recipes and household hints.
II. A major portion of magazines for women also contains items mentioned above.
Conclusions: I. Women are not interested in other things.
II. An average woman's primary interest lies in home, specially in the kitchen.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Cases of bride-burning for dowry are not uncommon.
Conclusions: I. Inspite of anti-dowry laws, the ill practice continues.
II. The punishment inflicted on the party concerned is insufficient.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Players who break various records in a fair way get special prizes. Player X broke the world record but was found to be under the influence of a prohibited drug.
Conclusions: I. X will get the special prize.
II. X will not get the special prize.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Inspite of the claim of the government that terrorism is under check, killing continues.
Conclusions: I. The terrorists have not come to an understanding with the government.
II. The government has been constantly telling a lie.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: National Aluminium Company has moved India from a position of shortage to self-sufficiency in the metal.
Conclusions: I. Previously, India had to import aluminium.
II. With this speed, it can soon become a foreign exchange earner.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Smoking is one of those human weaknesses which tends to test one's will-power to the edge.
Conclusions: I. It is very difficult for a smoker to give up smoking even if he wants to do so.
II. Human beings have other weaknesses as well.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: The country is absolutely self-dependent these days.
Conclusions: I. It is impossible to grow and produce all that a country needs.
II. The country men in general have become lazy.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Video libraries are flourishing very much these days.
Conclusions: I. People in general have got a video craze.
II. It is much cheaper to see as many movies as one likes on video than to see them in a cinema hall.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: About 50 per cent of animal by-products — hair, skin, horns etc. — is edible protein. American chemists have developed a method of isolating 45 per cent of this protein. They use an enzyme developed in Japan to break down Soya protein.
Conclusions: I. Americans have not been able to develop enzymes.
II. Animal by-products' protein has the same composition as Soya protein.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Domestic demand has been increasing faster than the production of indigenous crude oil.
Conclusions: I. Crude oil must be imported.
II. Domestic demand should be reduced.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: He stressed the need to stop the present examination system and replace it by some methods which would measure the real merit of the students.
Conclusions: I. Examinations should be abolished.
II. The present examination system does not measure the real merit of the student.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: The oceans are a storehouse of practically every mineral, including uranium. But, like most other minerals, it is found in extremely low concentrations - about three grams for 1000 tonnes of water.
Conclusions: I. Sea water contains gold.
II. The oceans harbour radiation hazards.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: I know nothing except the fact of my ignorance.
Conclusions: I. The writer's knowledge is very poor.
II. The world of knowledge is too vast to be explored by a single person.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: The devil can cite scripture for his purpose.
Conclusions: I. Even an unscrupulous person has some principles.
II. The real wisdom is to be found in scriptures.
